data.title: Iran attack lull tests whether Hormuz can reopen
data.description:
A sudden drop in Iranian attacks has sparked cautious optimism about de-escalation in the Middle East, though commercial shipping through the Strait of Hormuz remains severely disrupted.

Jim Bianco of Bianco Research flagged the dramatic shift in a social media post Thursday, noting that Iran had fired “just 7 drones today, all at Bahrain,” one day after launching 149 missiles and drones—a monthly high. 

The key question now, Bianco added, is if the lull will last and if it will be enough to “get ships moving.”

On that front, the picture remains bleak. Ship traffic through the strait is reportedly still well below 10% of normal levels despite the fragile ceasefire agreement, with only seven vessels passing in the past day compared to roughly 140 under typical conditions.

The backlog is expected to take weeks to clear, and Iran continues to tightly control how ships transit the waterway.

data.title: SA analyst upgrades/downgrades: PLTR, RIVN, GILD, INTC
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) and Rivian Automotive (RIVN) have both received upgrades, with Seeking Alpha analysts pointing to Palantir’s strengthening position as NATO’s modernization engine and Rivian’s transformation into a technology-platform play backed by major partnerships with Volkswagen and Uber.

On the downgrade side, Intel (INTC) has been moved to Neutral by analyst Uttam Dey, who believes the stock’s rally following its Tesla Terafab deal has priced in too much future growth. Gilead Sciences (GILD) also receives a Hold rating as recent acquisitions, while strategically sound, will take years to meaningfully contribute to revenue.

Upgrades

  • Palantir Technologies (PLTR): Upgrade Hold to Buy by Oliver Rodzianko. The analyst cites Palantir’s role as a core modernization engine for NATO and its deepening alliance architecture, with $4.4 billion in government deal value reinforcing its competitive moat.

    “The alliance angle is one that is strong for Palantir and also potentially undertapped. NATO itself has warned that weak modernization raises interoperability gaps, and Palantir also reported about $4.4 billion of government remaining deal value from the United States and allied countries, so part of the moat sits in coalition architecture.”

  • Rivian Automotive (RIVN): Upgrade to Buy by Ragmar Rikberg. The analyst highlights Rivian’s emergence as a compelling technology-platform play beyond traditional EV manufacturing, supported by an additional $2B in funding from Volkswagen and a potential $1.25B deal with Uber for up to 50,000 AV-ready vehicles.

    “The latest announcement about the Rivian–Uber collaboration adds, in my view, yet another strong pillar supporting Rivian’s bullish narrative...The $1.25 billion total package includes an initial $300 million equity investment, expected to follow shortly after signing, along with an agreement to purchase up to 50,000 AV‑ready vehicles if Rivian meets specific autonomous‑driving milestones by set deadlines.”

Downgrades

  • Intel (INTC): Downgrade to Neutral by Uttam Dey. While the foundry partnership with Tesla’s Terafab represents a long-term win, the analyst believes INTC shares have rallied too far, now trading at approximately 5x forward sales with flat near-term revenue and major Terafab contributions not expected until 2027.

    “I believe a majority of the future revenue that markets are excited about is already baked into the stock today...While Intel is leading the charge and moving ahead on its goals to deliver a sustained inflection, the resulting exuberance from the investor community is leading to Intel’s shares trading at rich valuation premiums.”

  • Gilead Sciences (GILD): Hold by Daniel Schönberger. The analyst maintains a cautious stance as recent acquisitions of Tubulis, Ouro Medicines, and Arcellx are strategically aligned but will take years to impact revenue, with the stock appearing fairly valued based on DCF analysis.

    “In my opinion, Gilead Sciences is a "Hold" at this point, and the recent acquisitions don't change the picture much. It is rather difficult to assess whether the acquisitions were good strategic decisions and will lead to higher growth rates.”

data.title: Lynas, Glencore, Rio Tinto top large-cap Materials picks by SA Quant ahead of earnings
data.description:
As interest in critical materials rises, Lynas Rare Earths Limited of Australia ranks highest among large-cap materials stocks. Its stock (LYSCF) has gained about 82% year to date, alongside other top-ranked mining giants Glencore (GLCNF) and Rio Tinto (RIO).

Therefore, as earnings season nears, investors are turning to updated quant ratings to gauge how companies stack up on valuation, growth, profitability, momentum, and estimate revisions after recent results.

State Street Materials Select Sector SPDR ETF (XLB), meanwhile, has climbed about 14% year to date, leading sector gains even as broader markets remain volatile.

Top-quant rated stocks:

  • Lynas Rare Earths Limited (LYSDY), Quant Rating: 4.96, Strong Buy.
  • Glencore plc (GLNCY), Quant Rating: 4.95, Strong Buy.
  • Rio Tinto Group (RIO), Quant Rating: 4.93, Strong Buy.
  • Lundin Mining Corporation (LUNMF), Quant Rating: 4.93, Strong Buy.
  • Coeur Mining, Inc. (CDE), Quant Rating: 4.91, Strong Buy.

Lowest quant-rated stocks:

  • DSM-Firmenich AG (DSFIY), Quant Rating: 1.85, Sell.
  • Symrise AG (SYIEY), Quant Rating: 2.05, Sell.
  • Givaudan SA (GVDNY), Quant Rating: 2.07, Sell.
  • Heidelberg Materials AG (HDLMY), Quant Rating: 2.09, Sell.
  • Akzo Nobel N.V. (AKZOY), Quant Rating: 2.10, Sell.

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), (RSPM)

data.title: ‘Iran doesn’t need to sink U.S. warships, it could inflict more damage by sinking the markets’ – strategist
data.description:
Dhaval Joshi, chief strategist at BCA Research, has identified a specific market threshold that could determine the trajectory of the U.S.-Iran conflict: a 12%-15% combined drawdown in stocks and bonds.

According to Joshi, this level represents U.S. President Donald Trump’s “breaking point” in both the current Iran conflict and last year’s tariff war.

“The markets now stand 7% above this breaking point,” the strategist noted, suggesting that a combined 7% selloff marks a good support level for the S&P 500 (SP500) and the 20+ Year Treasury Bond fund (TLT).

The strategist has framed Iran’s approach as deploying what he calls “General Stock Market” and “General Bond Market” as proxies for conventional warfare.

“Iran doesn’t need to sink a single U.S. warship – it could inflict much more damage by sinking the U.S. stock and bond markets,” Joshi argued in his analysis.

According to the strategist, this dynamic has created a pattern where every time an ultimatum for Iran nears, Trump has announced an “ultimatum extension” under market duress, only to later announce that insufficient progress has been made once markets recover.

The BCA Research chief explained that there are diverging interests driving Trump’s sensitivity to both stock and bond markets. His supporters primarily cares about surging gasoline prices (XB1:COM), inflation, and mortgage rates—all reflected in bond market performance—while having limited stock exposure, he said.

Meanwhile, Joshi noted that the U.S. economy has become “increasingly dependent on the spending of retired Baby Boomers,” whose wealth is tied to stock market performance.

In addition, the volatile environment has left investors struggling to apply traditional frameworks. Joshi referenced research describing how markets have “fluctuate like meme-coins in a game of Trump Tweet-AP Headline pong.”

Based on his analysis, if the iShares 20+ Year Treasury Bond ETF (TLT) fell 2%, it would equate to S&P 500 (SP500) support at 6,400, while a flat TLT would correspond to S&P 500 support at 6,250.

For investors seeking positions independent of the conflict’s outcome, Joshi recommends three trades: going long gold (XAUUSD:CUR) versus silver (XAGUSD:CUR), long Ethereum (ETH-USD), and underweight Japanese equities (EWJ).

The strategist warned that Japanese stocks’ (EWJ) outperformance has been fueled by the “sugar rush” of deeply negative real interest rates, putting the market at “huge risk of unwind” as inflationary pressures intensify regardless of how the Middle East conflict concludes.

Bond ETFs: (AGG), (BND), (VCIT), (MUB), (MBB), (JNK), (LQD), and (HYG).

Market ETFs: (DIA), (DDM), (DOG), (DXD), (SDOW), (SPY), (VOO), (IVV), (RSP), (SSO), (UPRO), (SH), (SDS), (SPXU), (QQQ), (QQQM), (TQQQ), (QID), and (SQQQ).

data.title: What’s next for health insurance stocks after the Medicare Advantage rate boost?
data.description:
Health insurance stocks surged following the Trump administration’s announcement that average Medicare Advantage payments will increase by 2.48%, or more than $13 billion, in 2027.

The news sent shares of major insurers sharply higher, with Humana (HUM) rising 7.9%, UnitedHealth Group (UNH) up 9.4%, CVS Health (CVS) climbing 6.7%, and Elevance Health (ELV) gaining 3.1%. The rate hike represented a dramatic reversal from the 0.09% increase proposed in January, which had pummeled the sector.

Despite the positive reaction, analyst sentiment remains mixed on whether this “Trump Bump” is enough to sustain a long-term rally in healthcare stocks amid persistent headwinds from medical cost inflation and elevated utilization.

What Do Seeking Alpha Analysts Say About Health Insurance Stocks’ Future?

Optimists pointed to the significant reversal in regulatory sentiment, noting that the finalized 2.48% boost removes the existential threat of flat funding risk that had weighed heavily on the sector. The $13 billion injection into the Medicare Advantage system for 2027 is expected to translate to meaningful margin expansion, with one analyst estimating $3.94 billion in incremental revenue and close to $1 billion in additional operating profit for UNH alone. 

Sellers, however, highlighted that the 2.48% increase still falls below medical cost inflation running at approximately 8-10%, suggesting margin pressures will persist. Some analysts noted that health insurers’ 2027 margin targets now appear increasingly difficult to achieve, with one describing the development as “not such bad news” rather than “great news.” The ongoing risk of the DOJ investigation into Optum’s billing practices and uncertainty around utilization trends also tempered enthusiasm.

Here’s a breakdown of what some analysts had to say:

  • Danil Sereda on UNH, Rating: Buy: “UNH's setup for the rest of this year looks much better with the most recent CMS news, in my view. The cost-cutting and margin-focused initiatives that the new management has been taking should be supported by higher MA payments now in 2027.” - UnitedHealth: The CMS Boost Changes Everything Now

  • Dilantha De Silva on UNH, Rating: Buy: “UNH can leverage the CMS windfall to enhance supplemental benefits, bid more aggressively, and potentially resume market expansion and buybacks as its debt-to-capital ratio improves.” - UnitedHealth: Quantifying The Impact Of The New CMS Payment Increase

  • James Foord on UNH, Rating: Strong Buy: “The market was getting overly pessimistic, which is why the recent news from the CMS has led to such a spike. Slowly, but surely, I expect UNH to recover to its previous level of valuation… UNH could really stand to gain market share now as other smaller players struggle with this tightened market.” - UnitedHealth Could Reprice Soon: The Catalyst Is Here

  • Stephen Ayers on CVS, Rating: Hold: “CVS’s diversity also provides a buffer against negative MA-related developments, making it a ‘safer’ option for income investors who want exposure to ‘beaten-down’ insurers… The 2.48% rate boost doesn’t reduce utilization risk. Nor does it make CVS a high-growth story. But it does improve the margin trajectory.” - CVS Health: A Diversified Income Play With Improving MA Rate Visibility For 2027

  • Far Horizon, Rating: Hold: “The agreed increase remains below expected medical inflation. The implication is that the 2027 targets look hard to hit. In my view, the positive development here is more of a ‘not such bad news’ event than a ‘great news’ event. I don’t see the justification for a continued rally in the share price at this point.” - Medicare Rates Up - What This Really Means For Elevance

What Do the Quant Ratings Say?

The major health insurers carry mixed quant ratings from Seeking Alpha’s system, reflecting strong profitability offset by challenging growth and momentum metrics.

UnitedHealth Group (UNH): UNH currently carries a Hold rating. The company’s valuation appears favorable with a price-to-sales ratio of 0.56, significantly cheaper than the sector median of 3.45. Profitability earns an A+ grade, with a net income margin of 3%, substantially higher than the sector’s 1%. However, growth receives a D- grade, with year-over-year EPS declining 15% versus the sector’s 11% growth. Momentum is weak at C-, with one-year price performance of -47%, significantly below the sector’s 5%.

Elevance Health (ELV): ELV is rated Hold, with strong profitability earning an A grade and a net income margin of 3%, exceeding the sector average. Valuation scores a B- with an attractive price-to-sales ratio of 0.34 compared to the sector median of 3.52. Growth receives a D- grade, with EBITDA declining 14% year-over-year. Momentum is weak at C-, with shares down 29% over the past year. Earnings revisions score a concerning D+ with 15 downward EPS revisions compared to only 3 upward in the past three months.

CVS Health (CVS): CVS carries a Hold rating with mixed performance across factors. Valuation earns a B+ grade with a low price/sales ratio of 0.23 compared to the sector median of 3.52. Profitability is strong at A+ with an Asset Turnover Ratio of 1.58, far outperforming the sector median of 0.47. Growth struggles at D+, particularly with EPS declining 62% year-over-year. Revisions score a C with 11 downward EPS revisions compared to 8 upward in the last three months.

What’s the Latest News on Health Insurance Stocks?

The CMS announced a finalized 2027 Medicare Advantage payment increase of 2.48%, injecting more than $13 billion into the system. This represented a dramatic reversal from the 0.09% rate hike proposed in January that had sent insurance stocks tumbling. CMS Administrator Dr. Mehmet Oz stated in the release that the updates “keep coverage affordable and ensure patients get real value from their plans.”

In addition to the rate increase, the CMS finalized a rule to eliminate nearly a dozen metrics in the Medicare Advantage Star Ratings system, a move expected to provide health insurers with more than $18 billion over the next decade. The changes boost quality bonus payments to insurers with four or more stars.

Analysts now expect insurers to submit more aggressive bids for 2027 by the June deadline, potentially using the revenue windfall to restore supplemental benefits and recapture lost market share.

data.title: U.S. stocks flirt with green territory as U.S.-Iran resolution is still uncertain
data.description:
Wall Street's major averages pared losses on Thursday as hopes of a resolution to the U.S.-Iran conflict waned following warnings from President Donald Trump of a major escalation.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last flat, while the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.1%, and the Dow (DJI) was -0.2%.

Over in the bond market, the benchmark 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was 1 basis point higher at 4.31%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) dropped 1 basis point to 3.79%.

Crude oil futures (CL1:COM) rose again and were last at around $101.5, while Brent (CO1:COM) was also higher at $98.9 per barrel.

“All U.S. Ships, Aircraft, and Military Personnel…and anything else that is appropriate and necessary for the lethal prosecution and destruction of an already substantially degraded Enemy, will remain in place in, and around, Iran, until such time as the REAL AGREEMENT reached is fully complied with,” Trump said on Truth Social.

As the U.S. agreed to a ceasefire in the Middle East, conditional on Iran agreeing to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, Tehran also reportedly agreed to the two-week deal as long as all attacks were stopped, according to Iran’s foreign minister.

However, Iran’s parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, has accused both the U.S. and Israel of violating three elements of the 10-point ceasefire deal, including continued attacks on Lebanon, a drone in Iranian airspace, and denying them "the right to enrich uranium."

In economic news, the February Core PCE Price Index came in at +0.4% MoM vs. +0.3% consensus and +0.4% prior. In addition, the PCE Price Index came in at +0.4% MoM vs. +0.4% consensus and +0.3% prior.

The initial jobless claims rose more than expected in the past week, while the U.S. Q4 GDP growth estimate was further revised down to +0.5%.

“We are certain that American central bankers will not [dismiss the latest economic data], given the 3.7% three-month average annualized pace of core inflation and the ugly inflation-adjusted income and savings dynamics inside the February report,” said Joseph Brusuelas, RSM U.S. principal and chief economist.

“While the topline increase of 0.1% and 2.8% is pre-war data and treated as stale, given the oil shock that will send it soaring in the March data, the increase in the core metric—the best long-run predictor of overall inflation—is far more problematic for central bankers that are now six years into a journey well above the Fed’s 2 percent inflation target, whether the ceasefire in the Gulf holds or hostilities resume in the near term,” he said.

data.title: SK Telecom is the top performing foreign communication services stock YTD
data.description:
Foreign communication services stocks have had a strong start to 2026, with telecom stocks broadly outperforming across international markets as investors rotate into defensive, yield-generating sectors amid broader equity uncertainty.

The diversified communication services industry has outpaced the S&P 500 over the past year, gaining 21.2% compared to the index's 17.6%, and several non-U.S. telecom names have exceeded even those benchmarks by a wide margin.

The following list ranks the top 10 foreign communication services stocks by year-to-date performance, drawing from South Korea, Luxembourg, Brazil, Italy, Finland, Mexico, Thailand, France, Sweden, and France, spanning wireless telecommunications, integrated telecom services, and cable and satellite industries.

The list is topped by SK Telecom Co., Ltd. (SKM), with a YTD performance of 67.56%. Millicom International Cellular S.A. (TIGO) and Telefônica Brasil S.A. (VIV) are next, with TIM S.A. (TIMB) and Elisa Oyj (ELMUY) rounding out the rest of the top five.

América Móvil, S.A.B. de C.V. (AMX) and Advanced Info Service Public Company Limited (AVIFY) follow closely behind. Orange S.A. (ORANY), Telia Company AB (TLSNY), and Eutelsat Communications S.A. (ETCMY) complete the top 10. The list features diverse representation across wireless telecommunication services, integrated telecommunication services, and cable and satellite industries.

Here is the list:

  1. SK Telecom Co., Ltd. (SKM), YTD perf: 67.56%, Quant Rating: Hold 3.47

  2. Millicom International Cellular S.A. (TIGO), YTD perf: 45.17%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.76

  3. Telefônica Brasil S.A. (VIV), YTD perf: 39.71%, Quant Rating: Buy 4.44

  4. TIM S.A. (TIMB), YTD perf: 38.82%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.81

  5. Elisa Oyj (ELMUY), YTD perf: 38.51%, Quant Rating: Not Covered

  6. América Móvil, S.A.B. de C.V. (AMX), YTD perf: 27.19%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.51

  7. Advanced Info Service Public Company Limited (AVIFY), YTD perf: 27.15%, Quant Rating: Not Covered

  8. Orange S.A. (ORANY), YTD perf: 26.18%, Quant Rating: Strong Buy 4.89

  9. Telia Company AB (TLSNY), YTD perf: 24.56%, Quant Rating: Not Covered

  10. Eutelsat Communications S.A. (ETCMY), YTD perf: 22.78%, Quant Rating: Not Covered

data.title: North Korea showcases new warhead, electronic weapons in latest tests
data.description:
North Korea said it tested a series of new weapons this week, including a ballistic missile equipped with a cluster-bomb warhead and an electromagnetic system, as it seeks to demonstrate capabilities suited for modern warfare.

State media reported that the trials also involved carbon-fibre bombs and a mobile short-range air defense system. Officials described the electromagnetic weapon and carbon-fibre munitions as key additions to the country’s arsenal.

South Korea’s military said Pyongyang had launched multiple missiles over several days, underscoring rising tensions on the Korean Peninsula.

Analysts said the tests appear aimed at highlighting advances in conventional weapons, particularly those designed to disrupt infrastructure and electronic systems, Reuters reported.

Electromagnetic weapons could potentially disable military hardware, while carbon-fibre bombs are intended to cripple power grids by spreading conductive material over targets.

North Korea also emphasized the use of low-cost materials in some tests, suggesting a focus on scalable production.

The developments come as diplomatic activity picks up in the region. China’s foreign minister is visiting Pyongyang, and there is speculation about a possible meeting between Donald Trump and Kim Jong Un in the coming weeks.

Experts say the weapons program reflects lessons drawn from recent conflicts and points to a strategy centered on asymmetric warfare, including attacks on industrial and energy infrastructure, Reuters reported.

data.title: AMC Networks rebrands as AMC Global Media for streaming future
data.description:
AMC Networks (AMCX) is rebranding as AMC Global Media, a pointer to the pay TV veteran's shift toward streaming as its major source of revenue.

The company filed the change as part of new articles of incorporation in Nevada.

Its ticker symbol won't change, though; it will continue to trade on Nasdaq as AMCX.

The news comes ahead of a high-profile weekend launch for the company: The Audacity, a new series set in Silicon Valley from Jonathan Glatzer.

“Our new name reflects the ongoing transformation of our business into a global media and studio-driven company, with streaming out front as our leading source of domestic revenue,” CEO Kristin Dolan said, according to The Hollywood Reporter. “AMC Global Media produces and curates high-quality content and distributes it to viewers around the world through a variety of platforms and partnerships.”

Earlier this week, the company's chief accounting officer, Michael J. Sherin III, took over as principal financial officer with the resignation of the company's CFO.

Last fall, the company re-signed Dolan to serve as CEO through 2028. She had taken the helm of the company in early 2023.
